title: Google, Twitter and 24 Other Tech Companies Get Lower Estimates by J.P. Morgan. But Amazon, Booking and Uber Remain Its Top Picks.

title: Booking Holding's Stock To Benefit As Borders Open Up Again
text: Booking.com and other travel websites have witnessed significant demand recovery, as travelers look to take advantage of the global opening since COVID.  The first quarter of 2022 saw an estimated 117 million travelers compared to 41 million travelers during the same quarter in 2022.

title: Why Booking Holdings Shares Are Trading Lower

title: T-Mobile Announces T-MobileTravel.com with Priceline for Exclusive Customer Travel Benefits

title: 10 Most Expensive Stocks In 2022: Amazon Is Off The List
text: With Amazon.com, Inc's (NASDAQ: AMZN) 20:1 stock split in the rearview, the company no longer ranks as the fourth most-expensive stock in the world. Shares closed on Friday with a value of $2,447 — and the markets opened Monday with shares priced at a mere $122.
